title: Polymarket Cross-Venue Signals Scientific Collaboration 34
thesis: Use Polymarket microstructure and cross-venue event matching to produce robust paper-only signal layers. This variant fuses microstructure researcher guidance with game theory/behavioral researcher, control/rl researcher to test order-book stress, queue pressure, and execution-quality asymmetry; opponent adaptation, crowd reflexivity, and exploitability of consensus breakdowns; feedback-aware thresholding and action policies that stay robust under execution drift.
guardrails: No live promotion without human approval.
No credentials or hard caps may be mutated.
